L1.area: Certificate and TLS resource management API layer

L2.description: A path traversal vulnerability exists in the certificate import feature where user-supplied file paths for certificate and key storage are passed directly to file-write operations without any validation or sanitization. An attacker can specify arbitrary file system paths to write attacker-controlled content to any location on the host, potentially leading to remote code execution if critical application configuration files are overwritten.

L3.area: SSL certificate import and modification API endpoints and the underlying certificate file persistence logic
L3.description: A path traversal vulnerability exists in the SSL certificate import feature where user-supplied file paths for certificate and key storage are passed directly to file-write operations without any validation or sanitization. An attacker can supply arbitrary file system paths via the certificate management REST API to write attacker-controlled content to any location on the host, potentially achieving remote code execution by overwriting the application's configuration file to change the startup command.

ground_truth.reason: The Import Certificate feature accepts arbitrary user-controlled file paths for SSL certificate and key storage and writes user-supplied content to those paths without any validation or sanitization, allowing an attacker to write to any location on the host file system.
